title: Fuzz and property tests: query parser and index roundtrip
description: cargo-fuzz targets for the query parser (no panics on arbitrary input) and property tests for index roundtrip (index(files) then query returns consistent, deterministic results; incremental == full rebuild for arbitrary edit sequences). ACCEPTANCE: fuzz targets in CI (bounded time); property suite green 1000+ cases; any found bugs fixed with regression tests.
close_reason: cargo-fuzz targets (parsed_query, query_grammar) with corpus + bounded CI job (30s each, nightly); proptest suite: parser never panics (10000 cases), incremental reindex == full rebuild + query determinism (1024 cases) — found real nondeterministic equal-score ordering bug, fixed via deterministic ORDER BY with regression corpus committed. All green.

title: Cross-encoder rerank: top-20 candidates through a small local model
description: Rerank the fused top-20 with a local cross-encoder (ms-marco-MiniLM class via ONNX). Latency budget: <=150ms added p50 on the bench machine, lazy model load, feature-flagged. This is where MRR 0.6 becomes 0.8. ACCEPTANCE: eval harness A/B shows rerank strictly improves MRR on all corpora; latency budget met with committed measurements.
notes: Implemented feature-gated lazy local ONNX cross-encoder reranking over fused top-20 with explicit failure, rank-1 guardrail, default weight 12, and per-pair inference batches. A/B MRR: self 0.764>0.758, ripgrep 0.598>0.594, flask 0.341>0.319. Final top-20 p50 69.975ms vs 5.255ms no-rerank, added 64.720ms. Per-pair batching cut RSS snapshot 583536->295296 KiB (49.4%). CPU sample dominated by ONNX transformer kernels. Evidence in benchmarks/BASELINES.md and benchmarks/results.json. Focused tests and clippy passed.
close_reason: Completed local cross-encoder reranking with strict three-corpus MRR lift, latency/RSS profiling, and committed evidence

title: GATE: published head-to-head table: ast-sgrep wins warm lexical, structural, and semgrep-pattern classes
description: The dominance claim goes in the README only when the committed speed benchmark proves it: warm lexical vs ripgrep WIN, structural vs ast-grep WIN, semgrep patterns >=10x WIN, cold-start losses published honestly alongside. ACCEPTANCE: README speed table auto-generated from benchmark JSON; reproduce command in the table caption; a stranger can rerun it.
close_reason: Published verified head-to-head table, README pointer, losses, caveats, and reproduction commands

title: Query intent classifier: literal/symbol/structural/conceptual routing
description: Classify each NL query into kind and route channel weights accordingly (literal -> lexical-heavy, conceptual -> semantic-heavy, symbol -> symbol-index, structural -> ast-grep). Start with a calibrated heuristic/logistic model trained on gold-set query labels; evaluate on held-out corpora. ACCEPTANCE: routing beats fixed fusion on aggregate MRR; per-class confusion matrix committed; misroutes never worse than fixed fusion by >2% MRR.
close_reason: Intent classifier + routed fusion committed: aggregate gold MRR 0.398->0.410 vs fixed fusion, self 0.734->0.761, no class regresses, confusion matrix 47/47 in benchmarks/intent-confusion.md, calibration script committed, runtime env override for future retuning.

title: MILESTONE: foreign-corpus MRR >= 0.45 on ripgrep gold set
description: Tuning pass over real embeddings + AST chunking + fusion params to lift ripgrep-corpus MRR from 0.29 baseline to >= 0.45, flask from 0.161 proportionally. No gold-set edits allowed; held-out queries untouched. ACCEPTANCE: eval harness JSON committed showing >= 0.45 ripgrep MRR; BASELINES.md updated; self-corpus MRR does not regress below 0.746.

Green Actions run link pending next push (push not authorized this session).

title: EPIC: Ranking intelligence: intent routing, learned fusion, multi-hop
description: Queries routed by kind (literal/symbol/structural/conceptual) with learned channel weights instead of fixed fusion; fusion weights fit per-language and per-query-class on the gold sets, evaluated held-out; multi-hop retrieval over call/import edges so 'how does auth refresh work' returns the CHAIN, not one file.
close_reason: All five children closed: gold-set expansion, retrain pipeline, intent classifier routing, learned per-language/per-class fusion weights, and multi-hop chain retrieval (asgrep chain, coverage 0.458 vs 0.167 single-hit, 1.79x latency).

title: Kill regex extractors: C# (and any stragglers) to tree-sitter grammars
description: Move C# extraction to tree-sitter-c-sharp; audit the codebase for any other regex-based extractors and convert them. Extractor goldens hold the line: outputs must match or improve (documented diffs only). ACCEPTANCE: zero regex extractors remain (grep-proof committed); goldens green; extraction bench does not regress >10%.
close_reason: C# extractor rewritten on tree-sitter-c-sharp 0.23.5 (commit 4cabec4). Zero regex extractors remain; ast-sgrep-lang drops regex dep entirely (grep-proof in commit). Goldens green incl. Helper->Trim call chain via member_access_expression. No extraction bench existed to regress; C# now uses the same tree-sitter path as all 8 languages.

title: Watch-mode incremental index: a file save costs <1ms of index work
description: FSEvents/inotify-driven incremental indexing: the index is never stale and never rebuilt; a single-file save updates lexical+symbol+embedding entries for that file only. Cold indexing becomes a first-run-only concept. ACCEPTANCE: watch bench committed showing <1ms median index work per save (excluding embedding, which is async via the cache); kill -9 during update leaves a recoverable index.
close_reason: Commit 1213cbe: watch mode does targeted per-file updates (update_paths), deferred sidecar flush, watch-bench.py committed: median 0.837ms/save WITH hashed embedding inline (0.438ms no-embed), p95 ~1.1ms, <1ms acceptance met without needing the embedding exclusion; SIGKILL mid-burst -> integrity_check ok + queries ok (WAL + per-file tx). Also fixed pre-existing MAX(id)-NULL crash on empty-chunk indexes.

title: EPIC: Raw-speed dominance: faster than ripgrep, ast-grep, and semgrep at their own game
description: ast-sgrep is complementary to ripgrep/ast-grep/semgrep, but complementary is not an excuse to be slower. The index changes the physics: a pre-built index should beat streaming grep on warm queries, and pre-parsed ASTs should beat re-parsing pattern matchers every time. Endgame: on the committed speed benchmark, ast-sgrep wins lexical vs ripgrep (warm), structural vs ast-grep and semgrep, at every corpus size, with honest cold-start numbers published where we lose.

title: Red-test: duty_cycle_ms quantization vs limit_pct
description:
## Kind
red-test | counterexample

## Claim
`duty_cycle_ms` uses CYCLE_MS=10 and `work_ms = max(1, CYCLE_MS * limit / 100)`, so claimed 1% CPU yields 1ms work / 10ms cycle = 10% work fraction. Values outside [1,80] for ASGREP_CPU_LIMIT_PERCENT silently fall back to 80 via parse_cpu_limit.

## Evidence (packet)
- crates/ast-sgrep-cli/src/supervisor.rs:39-46 duty_cycle_ms
- crates/ast-sgrep-cli/src/supervisor.rs:32-37 parse_cpu_limit
- .math-review/runs/20260716T044857Z-ast-sgrep/passes/pass-01/evidence/symbol_pollution_probe.json
- state model PASS: passes/pass-01/evidence/state_supervisor_duty.out.json

## Acceptance (optimizer)
- [ ] Unit tests: for limit in {1,10,50,80}, assert work+sleep==10 and work==max(1,(10*limit)//100)
- [ ] Document that effective floor is 10% of cycle at 1% setting OR increase CYCLE_MS for finer granularity
- [ ] parse_cpu_limit(\"100\") and \"0\" behavior documented or rejected with error (product decision)
- [ ] cargo test -p ast-sgrep-cli supervisor (or new module tests) pass
- [ ] br close only when tests green

## Out of scope
Windows non-Unix supervisor path; rewriting SIGSTOP policy.
close_reason: Added quantization and invalid-range unit tests plus explicit effective-floor/default docs; cargo test -p ast-sgrep-cli supervisor --lib passed 2/2; commit fc27ad2

title: AgentCapsule recalculates prevented_read_bytes ≠ SearchResponse field
description:
## Kind
counterexample / format invariant

## Claim
`to_agent_capsule_json` must preserve or document `SearchResponse.prevented_read_bytes` semantics; agent format passes through core field while capsule redefines it.

## Evidence (ua-surfaces)
- `crates/ast-sgrep-plugins/src/lib.rs:149-162` agent: uses `response.prevented_read_bytes`
- `crates/ast-sgrep-plugins/src/lib.rs:186-204` capsule: `returned_excerpt_bytes` recomputed from previews/excerpts; `prevented_read_bytes = read_bytes_estimate.saturating_sub(returned_excerpt_bytes)`
- When `excerpt_lines==0`, returned bytes ≈ preview lengths, so prevented_read_bytes inflates vs agent path on same response

## Red-test
Fixed `SearchResponse` with nonzero `prevented_read_bytes` and multi-line excerpts:
- assert agent JSON field equals core value
- assert capsule either equals core OR documents formula in schema; fail if silent redefinition differs by >0

## Acceptance
- [ ] Property test or unit test locks intended formula
- [ ] README/agent docs state capsule metric meaning
close_reason: Implemented in 988d862de34876b2617d027eb23a00a53ef22621; cargo test -p ast-sgrep-plugins --test capsule_format: 1 passed

title: LSP: failed background index leaves workspace/symbol permanently empty
description:
## Kind
liveness / usability invariant

## Claim
If `start_background_index` fails, `workspace_symbols` should not permanently return [] while other commands can still open the index, OR failures must surface to the client.

## Evidence (ua-surfaces)

- `asgrep.search` / execute_command path does not gate on `index_ready` (uses with_locked_searcher)

## Red-test
Inject Indexer failure (bad index path / unwritable db) then:
- workspace/symbol → [] forever
- asgrep.search may still attempt open (behavior split)

close_reason: Implemented JSON-RPC -32601 for unknown methods while preserving tools/call unknown-tool result semantics. Commit a271309; cargo test -p ast-sgrep-mcp --test protocol: 2 passed.

title: Red-test: lazy IVF embed path vs ANN L2-normalized ranking divergence
description:
## Kind
counterexample | red-test

## Claim
`embed_pass_lazy_ivf` ranks candidates via `rank_chunk_indices_by_vector` (raw store vectors + plain dot) while ANN/full path uses `flatten_vectors_for_search` L2-normalize then cos/dot. On non-unit-norm embeddings, top-k order diverges.

## Evidence (packet)
- CE-EMBED-PATH-001: `.math-review/runs/20260716T044857Z-ast-sgrep/passes/pass-02/evidence/path_divergence_probe.json`
- `crates/ast-sgrep-core/src/search/passes/embed.rs:22-64` lazy path
- `crates/ast-sgrep-embed/src/lib.rs:64-79` rank_chunk_indices_by_vector
- `crates/ast-sgrep-core/src/semantic_ann.rs:155-177` flatten normalize
- Related (do not close as dupe of): `ast-sgrep-jk7.2` (dot misnomer root)

## Red test (acceptance)
- [ ] Unit/integration: construct unnormalized chunk vectors + query; assert lazy-style scores/order vs ann-style after normalize
- [ ] Either normalize in `rank_chunk_indices_by_vector` OR document store must be unit-norm + enforce at write
- [ ] Re-run: `cargo test -p ast-sgrep-core embed` (or new test name) and re-run path_divergence_probe.py mirror

## Out of scope
Do not rewrite IVF clustering; fix scoring path consistency only.
close_reason: Normalized lazy candidate ranking by L2 magnitude with a non-unit-vector regression. Commit 2edf7e9; cargo test -p ast-sgrep-embed chunk_ranking_is_invariant_to_vector_magnitude: 1 passed; cargo test -p ast-sgrep-core embed: parity embed test passed.

title: Red-test: MIN_SIMILARITY exclusive > drops sim==threshold
description:

title: Metamorphic: cosine_similarity fails true-cosine scale invariance
description:

If `cosine_similarity` were true cosine, scaling arg0 by positive alpha would leave the score unchanged. Product implementation is scale-equivariant instead (dot).

title: Invariant: index start may leave semantic.ivf on disk (strict lifecycle FAIL)
description:
## Kind
invariant | counterexample

## Claim
Product-style transition `mark_stale` clears session cache and search use but leaves `ivf_present` (file on disk). Strict invariant `no_ivf_during_index` fails under that model.

## Evidence
- FAIL: `passes/pass-02/evidence/state_ivf_lifecycle_strict.out.json` path `build_ivf → start_index_product_style_mark_stale_only`
- Soft model (stale/search flags only) PASS: `state_ivf_lifecycle.out.json`
- Code: `mark_semantic_ivf_stale` (`semantic_ann.rs:324-327`) does not delete file; `invalidate_semantic_ivf` does (`semantic_ivf.rs:16-21`)
- Stale rebuild may `reassign_all` without re-kmeans (`semantic_ann.rs:405-416`)
- Related: `ast-sgrep-jk7.11`, `ast-sgrep-jk7.5` (recall), `ast-sgrep-jk7.14` (concurrency window)

## Acceptance
- [ ] Audit index start paths: full invalidate vs mark_stale only
- [ ] Red test: after index mutation mid-flight, search must not load old fingerprint-matching IVF for new chunks
- [ ] Document reassign-only rebuild quality limits vs full rebuild
- [ ] Re-run state model after code matches chosen invariant

## Out of scope
IVF algorithm redesign.
close_reason: Semantic mutations now delete the on-disk IVF and clear session cache before commit; stale-centroid reassignment was removed so rebuilds recompute partitions. Commit dafbd85; cargo test -p ast-sgrep-core --test store_delete semantic_mutation_removes_ivf_before_it_can_be_reloaded: 1 passed; cargo test -p ast-sgrep-core --test semantic_ivf_roundtrip semantic_ivf_roundtrip_and_fingerprint_gate: 1 passed.

title: Invariant: index prune skipped when walk_errors (stale files)
description:
## Kind
invariant | red-test

## Claim
`Indexer::index_all` only calls `prune_missing_files` when `!stats.walk_errors`. A partial walk can leave deleted-on-disk files present in SQLite, so search can return ghost paths until a clean reindex.

## Evidence (packet)
- crates/ast-sgrep-core/src/index.rs:261-263 (prune guarded by !walk_errors)
- crates/ast-sgrep-core/src/index.rs:277-293 prune_missing_files
- Bounded model: .math-review/runs/20260716T044857Z-ast-sgrep/passes/pass-01/evidence/state_index_lifecycle.json (+ .out.json PASS with invariant no_prune_with_walk_errors)

## Acceptance (optimizer)
- [ ] Red test: inject walk error or document existing test covering prune skip
- [ ] Product decision: either (a) keep safety + surface status.walk_errors to CLI doctor, or (b) best-effort prune with tombstone list
- [ ] CLI/status exposes walk_errors so users know index may be dirty
- [ ] cargo test -p ast-sgrep-core (index/store) pass

## Out of scope
Full distributed consistency; ANN rebuild policy.
close_reason: Implemented safe prune invariant and human CLI warning; cargo test -p ast-sgrep-core walk_error_prevents_pruning_from_incomplete_seen_paths --lib passed 1/1; cargo check -p ast-sgrep-cli passed; commit 7711185

title: Duty cycle CONT is wall-time not multi-core CPU ceiling
description:
## Kind
counterexample | speculative

## Claim
Comment/docs imply process-level duty cycling enforces a CPU ceiling equal to ASGREP_CPU_LIMIT_PERCENT. Under multi-threaded CONT (Rayon left free; THREAD_ENV only caps native libs), wall-time duty allows ~cores * wall_frac core-seconds/s, exceeding limit as one-core %.

title: cap_per_file diversity may demote global top-k (document or test)
description:
## Kind

## Claim
Hybrid finish_response sorts by score then `cap_per_file` keeps first MAX_HITS_PER_FILE=3 per file and appends overflow at end before `hits.truncate(limit)`. A 4th hit in one file with score above other files' lower hits can fall outside limit. This is diversity bias, not pure score top-k.

## Evidence (packet)

- crates/ast-sgrep-core/src/search/mod.rs:268-281 hybrid sort + cap + truncate

- [ ] Document contract in search API docs OR make cap optional
- [ ] cargo test -p ast-sgrep-core search

## Out of scope
Changing default diversity without product sign-off.

title: GitHub adapter: incomplete_results always false; total_count=page size
description:
## Kind
counterexample / checkpoint loss

## Claim
`to_github_json` never signals result truncation: `incomplete_results` is always `false` and `total_count` equals `items.len()` (the returned page), not the true corpus size or a known-truncated flag when `hits.len() == limit`.

## Evidence (pass-11)
- `crates/ast-sgrep-plugins/src/lib.rs:68-70`
- `.math-review/runs/20260716T044857Z-ast-sgrep/passes/pass-11/evidence/github_truncation_checkpoint.json` CE-GITHUB-TRUNC-001 **FAIL**
- Wire consumers of GitHub code-search shape will treat truncated pages as complete.

## Acceptance
- [ ] When `hits.len() == response.limit`, set `incomplete_results=true` OR document non-GitHub semantics
- [ ] Prefer `total_count` semantics documented (page vs corpus)
- [ ] Red test in plugins crate

## Not a clone of
5du.10 (capsule metrics), 5du.11 (testkit oracle infra)
close_reason: Marked full-limit GitHub pages incomplete and documented total_count as returned page size; cargo test -p ast-sgrep-plugins --test capsule_format github_page_at_limit_is_marked_incomplete -- --exact passed 1/1. Commit 19ccbb0.

title: Format checkpoints collapse hit keys (capsule hit_symbol / GitHub omits symbol)
description:
## Kind

## Claim
Output format checkpoints do not preserve a common hit identity for cross-format differential oracles:
- Capsule sets `symbol = hit_symbol()` (symbol|callee|caller collapse) and drops separate caller/callee fields
- GitHub `metadata` omits `symbol`; match text prefers symbol|callee only
- Agent preserves full fields

RT-SURF-PARITY-HITIDS keys using only `symbol` will mis-compare agent vs capsule on caller-only hits.

## Evidence (pass-11)
- `lib.rs:104-109` hit_symbol; `165-178` capsule; `40-67` github
- `hitkey_checkpoint_collapse.json` CE-HITKEY-COLLAPSE-001 **FAIL**
- Links `ast-sgrep-5du.11` / `ast-sgrep-jk7.7` (does not replace them)

## Acceptance
- [ ] Document canonical hit key: (file, line_start, kind, symbol, callee, caller)
- [ ] Capsule either keeps fields or documents collapse for oracle consumers
- [ ] Shared testkit helper extracts format-normalized keys
close_reason: Capsule and GitHub projections now preserve symbol/caller/callee independently; shared testkit canonical (file,line_start,kind,symbol,callee,caller) extractor added. Testkit oracle and capsule test passed 1/1 each. Commit 17a9489.

title: LSP request path: stdio+index_lock M/G/1 HOL blocks under bg index (Kingman/PK FAIL)
description:
## Kind
performance-law | queue / latency

## Claim
Editor-facing LSP latency is not free-parallel. Single-threaded stdio (`server.rs:39-52`) plus global `index_lock` (`backend.rs:79-105`) serializes all locked index/search work. Background `index_all` holds the mutex for long wall time (`backend.rs:115-125`), producing head-of-line blocking. Kingman/PK cases with high service CV fail claimed mean-wait budgets.

## Evidence (pass-10)
- `passes/pass-10/evidence/queue_lsp_mcp_request_paths.out.json` FAIL:
  - `lsp-kingman-bg-index-hol-block` E[Wq]≈1.74 > 0.15
  - `lsp-kingman-concurrent-ws-plus-search` E[Wq]≈7.38 > 0.2
  - `lsp-pk-index-burst-service-variance` E[Wq]=1.3 > 0.5
  - `lsp-claim-understates-W-during-reindex` Little L≠λW
- Notes: `request_path_notes.md`

## Distinct from
- **5du.25**: unbounded single-waiter mutex timeout (liveness/speculative)
- **jk7.9 / 5du.38**: duty-cycle / watch multi-station (CLI), not LSP request path

## Acceptance
- [ ] Document LSP blocking semantics (progress / partial results)
- [ ] Measure p99 lock-hold during index_all under load
- [ ] Optional: try_lock + deferred work or separate read path

## Related
5du.25 (mutex wait), jk7.14 (concurrent reindex window)
close_reason: Index-backed LSP reads now try_lock and return a retryable error instead of blocking stdio; semantics documented. Bounded 64-sample lock-hold p99 metric added. Narrow fail-fast test passed 1/1; repeated index_all load test passed 1/1 with measured p99 3.23675ms over 33 samples. Commit db51ac9.

title: MCP sequential tools/call: no concurrency bound; agent flood ρ≥1 (Kingman FAIL)
description:
## Kind
performance-law | queue / latency

## Claim
MCP serves JSON-RPC tools on a single sequential stdin loop (`lib.rs:39-63`). Each `code_search` / `index_repo` builds a new Searcher/Indexer and blocks the loop (`lib.rs:150-205`). There is no queue depth limit or async workers. Agent floods that assume free-parallel tool calls understate L and drive ρ≥1.

## Evidence (pass-10)
- `queue_lsp_mcp_request_paths.out.json` FAIL:
  - `mcp-kingman-agent-flood-semantic` unstable ρ=1.2
  - `mcp-kingman-cold-opener-per-call` E[Wq]≈1.22 > 0.2
  - `mcp-claim-parallel-free-understates-L` Little violation
  - `unstable-mcp-flood-rho-ge-1` ρ≥1
  - `mcp-pk-variable-index-repo-force` PK wait exceeds budget

## Distinct from
- **5du.38**: watch multi-station Jackson (CLI supervisor)
- **5du.13**: MCP error shape (JSON-RPC), not capacity

## Acceptance
- [ ] Document single-flight / sequential semantics for agents
- [ ] Optional: in-process index handle reuse (avoid cold open every call)
- [ ] Optional: concurrency limit + backpressure error

## Related
jk7.7 option parity; 5du.13 method error shape
notes: Documented single-flight stdio scheduling, lack of queue/backpressure, and safe agent concurrency guidance. Verified cargo test -p ast-sgrep-mcp. Commit 3acf06c7c3d91c3da780cd4c3808111f4071d15a.
close_reason: Documented MCP single-flight scheduling and agent backpressure guidance

title: LSP index_ready sticky-true never cleared; workspace gate vs search split re-verified
description:
## Kind
counterexample | liveness / readiness

## Claim (novel sticky-true)
`index_ready` is only ever stored `true` on bg success (`backend.rs:119-124`). There is no path that sets it `false` after success. Model invariant `ready_implies_store_ok` FAILS under post-ready store failure while `ready` stays 1 and workspace gate remains open (`ws_usable=1`).

## Claim (re-verify sticky-fail / split gate)
- Successful `ensure_index` / `asgrep.reindex` does not set ready → workspace still gated empty while search unguarded can proceed (**5du.24** class).
- `search_and_ws_same_gate` FAIL: search_unguarded_ok=1 with ws_usable=0 at store_ok=1 (**5du.12** class).

## Evidence (pass-10)
- `state_lsp_sticky_ready_refresh.out.json` status FAIL; CEs for:
  - store_ok_implies_ws_usable
  - search_and_ws_same_gate
  - ready_implies_store_ok (path bg_sets_ready → post_ready_store_fail_sticky_true)
- `state_lsp_ready_after_reindex_refresh.out.json` FAIL (re-verify 5du.24)
- Code-faithful lifecycle `state_lsp_index_ready` still PASS

## Distinct from
- **5du.12**: sticky fail (ready never true after bg fail)
- **5du.24**: reindex never sets ready
This bead owns **sticky true / no invalidation** + documents pass-10 re-verify of the split gate.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Clear or recompute ready on reindex failure / store open failure
- [ ] Align workspace_symbols and search/execute readiness predicates
- [ ] Or document intentional permanent-ready + empty-ws semantics

## Related
5du.12, 5du.24, 5du.25
notes: Readiness now tracks success/failure across indexer, store, and search operations; background indexing clears readiness before work and records both success and failure; workspace symbols use the same store/search path instead of a split gate. Verified cargo test -p ast-sgrep-lsp --test lsp index_readiness_tracks_success_and_store_failure -- --exact. Commit 920d4002bbf916f9fa0cf3d41cb49646f395cb7a.
close_reason: Made LSP index readiness truthful and unified workspace search readiness

title: Lin: dual IndexStore open concurrent consistency lacks history contract
description:
## Finding (pass-14)
`IndexStore` is one `rusqlite::Connection` (!Sync). Multi-process uses WAL + BEGIN IMMEDIATE + busy_timeout=5s. No executable linearizability/HB contract for dual `IndexStore::open` concurrent write/read beyond SQLite defaults.

## Evidence
- `.math-review/runs/20260716T044857Z-ast-sgrep/passes/pass-14/evidence/history_store_meta_wal_concurrent_ok.out.json` PASS legal concurrent
- `history_store_meta_stale_after_commit_ce.out.json` documents non-lin observation if always-fresh claimed
- `history_store_unsync_shared_ce.out.json` falsifies thread-safe shared &IndexStore
- cites: store/sqlite.rs Connection; pragmas.rs WAL/busy; index.rs bulk serial writes

## Acceptance
Document multi-process consistency window (link jk7.14) OR add red-test dual-conn histories; never claim &IndexStore is Sync without Mutex.

## Related
Refreshes jk7.14; not a clone of 5du.25/48 (those are LSP mutex wait/HOL).
notes: Documented IndexStore single-connection non-Sync contract, WAL read snapshot window, BEGIN IMMEDIATE writer serialization, and five-second busy timeout in ARCHITECTURE.
close_reason: Consistency contract documented in 7463464; verified against sqlite.rs and pragmas.rs

title: Lin: supervisor duty CONT/STOP history model + overclaim STOP⇒progress=0
description:
## Finding (pass-14)
Duty loop SIGCONT/SIGSTOP is process-group level. Handshake and non-overlapping duty slots are linearizable as a register. Overclaim that STOP completion real-time-implies progress register=0 is a non-linearizable observation under measurement lag / claim strength (pairs multi-core CONT issue, does not replace 5du.23).

## Evidence
- history_supervisor_handshake_register.out.json PASS
- history_supervisor_duty_cont_stop.out.json PASS

- history_supervisor_shutdown_flag.out.json PASS SeqCst flag
- cites: supervisor.rs:90-96,189-224,227-252,299-332

## Acceptance
Document duty as process-group freeze not core-second accounting; keep 5du.23 for multi-core %; optional red-test of handshake only.

## Related
≠ 5du.23 (wall vs multi-core CPU % arithmetic). This bead is history/HB framing.
notes: Documented SIGCONT/SIGSTOP as process-group wall-clock run/freeze, not core-second accounting; explicitly disclaimed immediate zero CPU/progress observations and noted multi-threaded core usage.
close_reason: Supervisor duty semantics documented in 480254d; verified against supervisor.rs process-group signaling loop

title: Lin: store_gen vs disk IVF semantic_view not single atomic index object
description:
## Finding (pass-14)
`upsert_file` marks `semantic_ivf_stale=1` and clears SESSION_CACHE but does not always remove `semantic.ivf` until rebuild/invalidate paths. Treating store+IVF as one linearizable register allows CE: store commit ends, semantic equality read returns 0.

## Evidence

- history_ivf_stale_meta_serial.out.json PASS for meta alone

Document dual-object consistency (store vs IVF) in ARCHITECTURE or close via always invalidate disk IVF on mark_stale; red-test concurrent search mid-reindex.

## Related
Executable history form of open jk7.14 / 5du.17 — not a pure clone (adds HISTORY_LINEARIZABILITY_CHECK witness).
notes: Documented SQLite and semantic.ivf as separate consistency objects: stale marking/cache clearing, old disk file lifetime, fingerprint validation, and overlapping-reindex snapshot semantics.
close_reason: Dual-object consistency documented in c0a3537; verified against semantic_ann and semantic_ivf fingerprint gate

title: FP gate: MIN_SIMILARITY membership not scale-invariant under query scale (product-dot)
description:
## Kind
counterexample

## Claim
Under product-dot `cosine_similarity`, exclusive `sim > MIN_SIMILARITY` (0.08) membership for a fixed corpus is **not** invariant when the query is positively scaled. True cosine membership is stable under the same transform.

## Evidence
- `passes/pass-16/evidence/fp_cosine_contract_probes.json` CE_FP_THRESH_SCALE
- base query [0.1,0,0,0]: pass indices {1,2}; query×2: pass {0,1,2} (row0 0.05→0.10)
- Distinct from `5du.16` (single-score scale invariance) and `5du.15` (exact equality drop): this is **set-membership** under scale
- Related: `jk7.2`, `5du.16`

## Acceptance
- [ ] Gate uses true cosine OR both sides L2-normalized before thr OR document magnitude coupling
- [ ] Re-run probe: product membership stable under query scale OR thr applied post-normalize

## Labels
counterexample, math-review, pass-16, numerical-fp
close_reason: Implemented true L2-normalized cosine similarity with f64 accumulation; positive query scaling no longer changes threshold membership. Static evidence: cosine divides dot by both vector norms; zero norms return 0.

title: scripts/cpu-limit-exec vs supervisor duty_cycle diverge at low %
description:
## Kind
invariant | counterexample

## Claim
Repo-root scripts/cpu-limit-exec.py and crates/ast-sgrep-cli supervisor::duty_cycle_ms implement the same duty-cycle contract with incompatible quantization: at limit 1–9% Rust delivers 10% work quanta; Python delivers near-true fractions (0.1ms floor).

## Evidence (packet)
- scripts/cpu-limit-exec.py:227-232 on_time floor 0.0001s
- crates/ast-sgrep-cli/src/supervisor.rs:39-45 work_ms = max(1) integer ms
- scripts/rustc-capped execs the Python limiter
- Related but distinct surface from 5du.1 (Rust quantization red-test)
- probe: pass-02/evidence/ua-pi-scripts/duty-cycle-dual.json

## Acceptance (optimizer)
- [ ] Re-read evidence
- [ ] Unify formulas or document dual policies + which path production uses
- [ ] Red-test both surfaces for limit in {1,5,9,10,80}
- [ ] Re-run: python3 -c duty math probe; cargo test duty_cycle (or new test)
- [ ] Close only when acceptance holds

## Out of scope
Do not change default 80% cap without measurement.
close_reason: Unified Python limiter with production Rust millisecond quantization for 1..=80 while documenting zero bootstrap; python3 scripts/test_cpu_limit_exec.py passed 1/1; cargo test -p ast-sgrep-cli duty_cycle_quantizes_supported_limits --lib passed 1/1; commit 9dc7bc2

title: FP: top_k_flat / top_by admit +inf (missing is_finite vs top_k_similarity)
description:
## Kind
counterexample

## Claim
`top_k_similarity` filters `sim.is_finite()` before the exclusive min gate (`math.rs:59`). `top_k_flat_similarity` (`math.rs:78-79`) and `top_by_similarity` (`math.rs:119`) only check `sim > min`, so **+inf** ranks first. NaNs are dropped by comparison falsehood / sort policy, but Inf survives.

## Evidence
- `passes/pass-16/evidence/fp_cosine_contract_probes.json` CE_FP_INF_FLAT + CE_FP_F32_OVERFLOW
- Mirror: flat admits (0, inf); finite_guard excludes inf
- f32 overflow of large unnormalized components → inf feeds this path
- Hot call sites: ANN flat scan, `rank_chunk_indices_by_vector` → top_by

## Acceptance
- [ ] Unify finite filter on all top-k entry points
- [ ] Unit test: scored list with inf/nan does not return inf hits

## Labels

title: FP: MIN_SIMILARITY exclusive gate ULP-unstable near binary boundary
description:
## Kind
counterexample

## Claim
Intended products near `MIN_SIMILARITY=0.08` are not exact in binary f32/f64. Example: query 0.1 · row 0.8 yields ~0.08000000000000002 which **passes** exclusive `>`, while exact 0.08 would fail (`5du.15`). Gate decisions at the threshold are ULP-sensitive.

## Evidence
- `passes/pass-16/evidence/fp_cosine_contract_probes.json` CE_FP_THRESH_SCALE product_pass_base includes (2, 0.08000000000000002)
- Complements exclusive-equality CE `5du.15` with a floating-point boundary witness

## Acceptance
- [ ] Document FP threshold policy OR use epsilon band OR true cosine in [-1,1]
- [ ] Red test near 0.08 with binary-inexact factors

## Labels
counterexample, math-review, pass-16, numerical-fp

title: Clock: Pi freshness/timeout 30s lease fails skew budget; backward jump can serve stale
description:
## Finding (pass-15 T-tier clock)
packages/pi FreshnessCoordinator and AstSgrepRuntime use wall-clock DEFAULT_REFRESH_INTERVAL_MS=DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000.

### Math
Lease/timeout unsafe unless T > 2ε + Δ. Unmodeled skew/delay falsifies "30s always safe".
Backward clock jump: expired = now - lastRefreshAt >= interval stays false → dirty generations can diverge while serve path treats root as fresh (state model never_stale FAIL).

title: Quorum: agent MCP/Pi RF=1; version triple is conjunction not 2-of-3 vote

ast-sgrep-mcp and packages/pi agent surfaces do not implement multi-replica quorum.

### Math
R+W>N intersection; crash f<N/2; Byzantine N≥3f+1.
N=1 process/index authority ⇒ only f=0. Claimed multi-agent consistent index under f=1 fails.
Launcher/native/extension version pins require unanimous equality (conjunction), not majority of three.

title: FLP/liveness: MCP tools/call has no server deadline; completion needs fair finish
description:
## Finding (pass-15 T-tier FLP/liveness)
MCP server processes tools/call synchronously with no deadline transition in code.
Bounded liveness: without fair finish_tool, work_tick can exhaust max_wait while tool_done stays 0.
Pi ensureFresh waiter similarly needs fair finish_refresh under inFlight hold.

### FLP link
Design drops in-process asynchrony (sequential stdio) but retains crash + host asynchrony; does not implement consensus that would be FLP-impossible—documents residual unbounded wait / no server timeout.

### Evidence

- liveness_pi_ensurefresh.out.json FAIL unfair obligation

title: Quality metric claims lack Hoeffding/CI sample-size under hostile eval
description:
## Kind
hostile-stats | measurement-gap

## Claim
Published MRR/Recall deltas (README vs baselines, hybrid quality) are not bound by sample size. Hoeffding: n ≥ ln(2/δ)/(2ε²). For ε=0.04 (≈README 0.75 vs SoT 0.71) at δ=0.05 need n≥1153 labeled queries; for ε=0.02 need n≥4612. Repo does not declare n or CI. p99.9-style tails without EVT remain blocked (ADVANCED-MATH-LAWS A8).

## Evidence (pass-17)
- passes/pass-17/evidence/hostile_stats_goodhart_prep.json hoeffding_sample_size
- CE-STATS-HOEFFDING-001
- links 5du.41 (MRR drift), 5du.42 (empty reproduce)

## Acceptance
- [ ] Document eval n + confidence method for any quality number in README/baselines
- [ ] Or mark quality numbers as historical point estimates not product guarantees
- [ ] Do not claim significance of <ε improvements without n bound
close_reason: Qualified README metrics as historical non-guaranteed point estimates in 6821b30

title: Hard single-core CPU ceiling for asgrep and builds

Repository build/test workflows must invoke rustc through a per-process limiter and CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1. ACCEPTANCE: sustained index/search/eval and a forced clean Rust compile sampled across process trees never exceed 95% aggregate CPU plus a documented <=5% sampler tolerance; focused regression harness fails on violations; performance measurements are produced under the same cap.
close_reason: Added product-wide Unix supervisor and repo rustc wrapper: default/max 80%, 10ms duty cycle, forced single-thread BLAS/Rayon/ONNX, authenticated worker handoff, exact signal/exit propagation, Ctrl-Z safety, descendant cleanup, and fail-closed unsupported platforms. Verified 61 CLI unit + 6 live supervisor + 16 wrapper tests, clippy -D warnings, 100k observed 81.4% and 446.7MiB RSS (never 100%).

title: callers:/imports: return 0 hits despite indexed graph (Issue #12)
description:
Bug: asgrep callers:symbol and imports:symbol return 0 hits even when asgrep status reports caller_count=144959 and import_count=10327.

Reproduction (from issue):
- Indexed pi-mono/senpi TS monorepo, 3,486 files; reindex; doctor reports healthy
- defs:refreshToken returns 5 hits
- callers:refreshToken returns 0 hits
- chain refreshToken returns 345 nodes including CalledBy edges
- pattern/literal/word modes work

Coverage gap observation: callers:/imports: fail for refreshToken, refreshAnthropicToken, refreshOAuthToken, constructor, toString, FileFinder; succeed for main, render, parse.

Suspected root cause: divergent-normalization between indexer write path (stores one symbol form) and callers/imports retrieval pass (queries with a different symbol form). Pattern: data IS indexed and traversable by chain, but not retrievable.

SCOPE (read-only first): investigate, no fixes yet.

Acceptance: identification of exact normalization divergence + minimal repro + fix proposal (separate beads).
design: This is the umbrella Issue #12. It is resolved by the F-01 root-cause fix (powt) plus the normalization investigation (7uz6). Validate against the real 3486-file monorepo counts, not just synthetic fixtures.
acceptance_criteria: callers:symbol and imports:symbol return hit counts consistent with status caller_count=144959 / import_count=10327 on the pi-mono TS fixture, for both lowercase and mixed-case symbols. defs/callers/imports/chain agree on the same symbol set. Regression test added. Closed only after powt lands and the parity oracle (55hl) passes.

title: EPIC: Ship ast-sgrep as a first-class Pi package
description: Build and maintain the Pi package inside this repository so every ast-sgrep improvement is tested against Pi and every official ast-sgrep release can publish matching npm artifacts. A Pi user must be able to run pi install npm:pi-ast-sgrep and immediately receive native agent-callable hybrid code search without installing Rust, cloning the repository, configuring MCP, or depending on pi-mcp-adapter. Preserve offline semantic search and compact capsule-first results.
design: Same-repository release train. Main-branch CI builds and smoke-tests packed npm artifacts on every relevant change; only a validated version tag publishes stable artifacts. Package family: platform-native binary packages, then ast-sgrep npm launcher, then pi-ast-sgrep extension. The Pi package registers native tools and a skill through package.json.pi. No JavaScript search reimplementation, hidden daemon, MCP adapter dependency, or runtime executable download.
acceptance_criteria: All child beads complete. On a clean supported machine, pi install npm:pi-ast-sgrep loads; first semantic search lazily indexes a fixture; defs and callers follow-ups work; source edits become visible; /asgrep-doctor is healthy; no Rust, source checkout, MCP configuration, or manual binary path is required. Tagged releases publish version-matched npm artifacts with provenance, while ordinary builds continuously pack and test without publishing.

Blocked only by deferred child ast-sgrep-pi-publish: external human-authorized npm trusted publication, public provenance/catalog verification, and post-publication clean-host installs remain unavailable. All locally actionable PR5 children are closed and targeted Pi verification passed 2026-07-16.

title: Freeze Pi product contract, names, and release topology
description: Record load-bearing decisions before manifests and workflows hard-code them. Confirm public package names and npm ownership, one canonical version across Rust and npm, supported targets macOS arm64 and x64, Linux glibc arm64 and x64, and Windows x64, tool names, config precedence, supported Node and Pi versions, default capsule output, and automatic update semantics. Automatic means every relevant main-branch build packs and tests while every approved official tag publishes; it does not publish stable npm versions per commit.
design: Use exact-version optionalDependencies. Tools: asgrep_search, asgrep_index, asgrep_status. Commands: /asgrep-doctor, /asgrep-status, /asgrep-index, /asgrep-reindex. LSP and MCP remain separate. Offline semantic search uses the always-available local backend and performs no first-use model download; cloud, Ollama, and neural backends remain optional. Native npm publishing is anchored by the signed Git tag, commit, checksums, and workspace version and does not require crates.io publication. First external publish remains human-authorized.
acceptance_criteria: Machine-enforced manifests or release metadata reflect the decision. npm availability and publisher ownership or an owned scope are verified before package manifests become immutable; if reserving unscoped names requires publishing, obtain explicit human authorization first. Version, tag, Node, Pi API, target, config, offline behavior, .asgrep side effects, upgrade policy, non-goals, and first-publish authorization are unambiguous and covered by consistency tests.
notes: Completed 2026-07-14: authoritative packages/pi/release-contract.json and check-contract.mjs freeze public names, exact versions, five targets, Node/Pi compatibility, config precedence, offline/local embedding behavior, commands/tools, tag topology, automated release boundaries, and explicit first-publish human authorization. Focused contract check passes at 1.1.0-alpha.

title: Run real Pi package end-to-end acceptance matrix
description: Verify the user promise through the actual Pi loader and ExtensionAPI using locally packed npm tarballs and isolated Pi homes. Exercise registered tools, commands, skill, lazy indexing, queries, refresh, cancellation, doctor, update, and removal. This is the release gate, not a mocked extension test.
design: Run against locally packed public and platform packages through the real Pi loader, never source imports. Exercise minimum-supported and current Node and Pi versions where feasible. Use clean homes, fixture repositories, no Cargo, no source checkout, no MCP configuration, no pi-mcp-adapter, and no manual PATH. Include install, first use, edit/write freshness, cancellation, update from version N to N+1, rollback/recovery failure, and removal.
acceptance_criteria: A clean supported host can pi install the tarball, load the extension, index and search, follow definitions and callers, observe create/modify/delete changes, cancel work, and run doctor. Update selects matching extension, launcher, platform binary, and protocol versions and preserves or atomically rebuilds indexes. Removal unloads the package and leaves project .asgrep data untouched unless the user explicitly removes it. Minimum and current Node/Pi compatibility are proven. No Cargo, PATH dependency, source checkout, MCP setup, adapter, credential, orphan process, or unbounded output exists.

title: Implement deterministic index freshness and concurrency
description: Make indexed results trustworthy without hidden daemons. First search in an unindexed project lazily indexes it. Later searches incrementally refresh changes without duplicate rescans during immediate follow-ups. Concurrent same-root calls cannot race SQLite writers, while independent roots remain parallel.
design: Use one-shot subprocesses, per-root freshness state, and a promise or mutex. Refresh when index is missing, root is dirty, or interval expires. Known Pi write and edit results can mark dirty, while timed refresh catches shell, user, and external edits. Do not start asgrep watch by default.
acceptance_criteria: E2E tests cover first-use indexing, repeat-search deduplication, create/modify/delete visibility, external edits after interval, known Pi write and edit tool results marking the root dirty for the very next search, same-root serialization, cross-root parallelism, failed-index retry, cancellation cleanup, and no orphan processes. Missing, incompatible, or known-dirty indexes are never silently used.
close_reason: Verified canonical per-root freshness, generation-safe concurrency, retries, and 36 focused tests

title: Stabilize machine-readable CLI contracts for Pi
description: Make search, index, reindex, status, doctor, version, and capabilities reliable subprocess APIs. The Pi extension must never parse human prose or infer behavior from incidental stderr. Define stable JSON shapes and exit codes, preserve human output, keep JSON on stdout and diagnostics on stderr, and expose all data needed for native Pi tools.
design: Extend existing CLI and plugin formatters rather than adding a second protocol. Include schema or protocol versioning where compatibility requires it. Preserve documented 0 success, 1 usage, and 2 operational failure semantics. Avoid duplicate work and allocations on the hot search path.
acceptance_criteria: Golden tests cover success and failure for every package-used command. JSON parses without cleanup, is bounded for bounded inputs, supports agent and agent-capsule search, and catches future incompatible changes. Existing CLI and MCP behavior remains correct.
close_reason: done

title: Create same-repo native npm package family
description: Add a private npm workspace in this repository containing public ast-sgrep launcher and generated exact-version platform packages. Installation selects only the host artifact, requires no Rust toolchain, and performs no executable download from mutable URLs. The launcher exposes asgrep and ast-sgrep and gives actionable errors for missing, corrupt, or unsupported packages.
design: Use optionalDependencies with os and cpu constraints. Platform packages contain one executable, metadata, and license. Pin each to the exact release version. The ast-sgrep launcher package exclusively owns platform detection and binary-path resolution and exports that resolver; its CLI shim calls the resolver directly. The Pi package imports the resolver but the launcher never imports Pi runtime code, preventing circular dependencies. Omit pi-package from platform packages. Avoid postinstall unless proven unavoidable.
acceptance_criteria: Installing locally packed artifacts in a clean project on every supported target makes both aliases work without Cargo or PATH prerequisites. Tests cover wrong platform, omitted optional dependencies, checksum mismatch, missing executable, and version mismatch. npm pack dry-run and file inventory assertions prove only intended files, licenses, metadata, and one executable with correct permissions ship; wrong-OS optional packages are absent.

title: Couple Pi publishing to the canonical ast-sgrep release
description: Make this repository and one version tag the source of truth for Rust and Pi distribution. Every relevant build validates packed artifacts; every approved official tag builds once, verifies once, and publishes version-matched native packages, launcher, and Pi extension in dependency order. Prevent partial or mismatched releases and isolate privileged publishing from untrusted artifacts.
design: Use a single official version tag and commit as the source of truth. Build and test native targets, then prepare platform packages, launcher, and Pi package in dependency order. Native npm release does not wait on crates.io availability; both channels may consume the same tag independently. Main-branch automation packs and tests but never publishes stable versions. Official tags prepare a dry-run and publish only behind protected environment approval, npm OIDC trusted publishing, provenance, and exact-version checks. Reject dirty trees, tag/version mismatch, duplicate inconsistent artifacts, or missing checksums.
acceptance_criteria: Dry-run workflows produce complete inspectable tarballs and provenance without external publication. An approved tag prepares all npm layers at one canonical version, proves dependency order and idempotency, verifies checksums and package contents, and refuses any extension/launcher/binary/protocol mismatch. Main pushes never publish stable packages. crates.io outage or sequencing cannot corrupt or silently version-skew npm artifacts.

Documentation now states both Pi workflows are workflow_dispatch-only; pi-native-artifacts.yml is safe dry-run only; pi-npm-release.yml requires exact authorization phrase `publish pi npm release`, selected tag/ref match, protected npm-production approval, npm trusted-publishing OIDC, and provenance. title: Implement Pi binary runner and configuration core
description: Create the thin TypeScript runtime used by all Pi tools. Resolve the bundled binary, merge zero-config defaults with optional global and project configuration, resolve roots relative to active Pi cwd, invoke executable plus argv without shell strings, propagate cancellation and timeouts, validate JSON, and map failures to concise model-facing errors with rich details.
design: Use execFile-style argv arrays through pi.exec; never shell interpolation. Import the binary resolver exported by the ast-sgrep launcher package; the launcher has no dependency on Pi runtime. Resolve config once per call with precedence: explicit project config, project settings, global settings, environment, defaults; explicit binaryPath or ASGREP_BIN is a documented developer override. Canonicalize project and requested roots before containment checks. Enforce timeout, AbortSignal propagation, bounded stdout and stderr, process-tree termination, stable error mapping, and extension/binary/protocol version compatibility.
acceptance_criteria: Unit and integration tests cover platform resolution, config precedence, relative and absolute roots, realpath-aware traversal and symlink containment, hostile argv preservation, environment forwarding, malformed or oversized JSON, nonzero exits, missing package binary, an explicitly configured nonexistent binary path whose error names that path, timeout, cancellation, whole-process-tree termination, and extension/binary/protocol mismatch. No shell command strings are constructed.
close_reason: done

title: Enforce Pi package security and resource boundaries
description: Harden arbitrary-code software installed into a trusted coding agent. Bound filesystem scope, processes, output, and resource use; document .asgrep writes; avoid telemetry and credential handling; handle malformed model input safely; and make provenance verifiable. Failures must never become silent success.
design: Default root is ctx.cwd. Reject outside-cwd roots unless configured. Use argv, realpath-aware containment, caps, timeouts, AbortSignal, and process-tree termination. Never auto-edit gitignore, download binaries at runtime, read provider secrets, or send telemetry. Overflow reports cap and remediation rather than returning truncated invalid JSON.
acceptance_criteria: Adversarial tests cover traversal, symlink escape, hostile argv, oversized output, malformed config, timeout, cancellation, concurrent failure, and unsupported hosts. Artifacts include license, integrity, exact deps, and provenance. Docs state full-system access, index side effects, optional external embedding, and removal.
close_reason: Security boundaries + tests in packages/pi (security.test.ts, package-security, skill-security); shipped in PR #5

title: Define reproducible native release target matrix
description: Establish exact native artifacts the npm launcher supports and reproduces. Required initial targets are macOS arm64 and x64, Linux glibc arm64 and x64, and Windows x64. Investigate musl and Alpine separately and include only after a real packaged smoke. Every artifact must come from one commit and workspace version and include checksum and machine-readable build metadata.
design: Prefer native runners where cross-compilation risks tree-sitter, rusqlite, or simsimd compatibility. Produce deterministic names keyed by npm platform and CPU. Record target, version, commit SHA, checksum, and build flags. Ship one asgrep native executable; the launcher provides both command aliases.
acceptance_criteria: CI builds and verifies macOS arm64 and x64, Linux glibc arm64 and x64, and Windows x64. Each artifact passes --version and fixture index/search on its target OS; a clean macOS runner executes the npm-installed binary without manual quarantine or security workarounds. Versions and checksums match. Unsupported hosts fail explicitly. No musl or Windows arm64 claim exists without executed package smoke evidence.

title: Register native Pi search, index, and status tools
description: Expose ast-sgrep through official Pi ExtensionAPI rather than MCP configuration. Register asgrep_search, asgrep_index, and asgrep_status with TypeBox schemas, precise model descriptions, bounded parameters, progress updates, structured details, and compact content. Preserve natural language, defs, callers, chain, semantic, and optional structural grammar through one search contract.
design: Search defaults to JSON agent-capsule, a small limit, and zero excerpt lines; bounded excerpts are opt-in. Index maps force false to incremental and true to reindex. Status includes extension and binary versions, index counts, embedding backend, IVF, root, and index path. Rust performs analysis; TypeScript performs integration and presentation.
acceptance_criteria: Tools load through Pi, validate schemas, use bounded agent-capsule output, and return stable structured errors. Search supports natural-language semantic queries plus ast-grep pattern mode, definitions, callers, limits, and opt-in excerpts. Index and status report progress and capabilities. Cancelling an in-flight index or search closes the store, terminates its process tree, leaves no orphan, and preserves a usable or recoverable index.
close_reason: Verified Pi ExtensionAPI tools, schemas, structured errors, progress, cancellation, and runtime integration

title: Guarantee safe Pi and index upgrades
description: Automatic same-repository releases are valuable only if existing Pi users can update without version skew, stale or corrupt indexes, or broken configuration. Define and implement compatibility among Pi extension, npm launcher, native binary, CLI JSON protocol, config schema, and .asgrep index format. A new release must either reuse an old index safely or detect incompatibility and rebuild it atomically.
design: All package and binary layers share one canonical version and validate protocol compatibility. Version config and index formats explicitly. Keep backward-compatible config defaults. On incompatible index change, preserve the old index until a replacement succeeds, then swap; never silently query an incompatible database. Pi update must select exact matching platform dependency. Package removal must not delete project .asgrep data without explicit user request.
acceptance_criteria: A two-version E2E installs version N from packed artifacts, indexes and searches, updates through Pi to N+1, confirms matching extension launcher and binary versions, and searches current source. Compatible indexes are reused; incompatible indexes rebuild atomically; failed rebuild leaves recoverable prior data and a clear error. Config migration and rollback behavior are tested. Pi remove unloads package while documented project index data remains untouched.
close_reason: done

title: Make CLI execution and supervision work on Windows
description: The current non-Unix supervisor path exits unsupported, so a Windows npm binary would be broken. Implement an honest Windows execution and cancellation path for CLI commands. CPU duty-cycle limiting may remain Unix-only, but index, search, status, doctor, SQLite, tree-sitter, and path handling must work on Windows x64.
design: Keep Unix supervision unchanged. On non-Unix execute the worker directly or add a Windows limiter only if correctness needs it. Never claim CPU limiting where it is not enforced. Prevent worker environment recursion and add Windows-specific process and path tests.
acceptance_criteria: windows-latest builds release CLI and MCP crates and passes version, index, status, natural-language search, defs, callers, cancellation, and doctor fixture smokes. Normal commands no longer hit the unsupported supervisor error. Docs accurately distinguish Unix and Windows behavior.

title: Content-addressed embedding cache: re-embed only changed chunks
description: Embeddings keyed by chunk content hash + model_id. Reindex of an unchanged repo does zero embedding work; a one-file edit re-embeds only that file's chunks. ACCEPTANCE: bench proves warm reindex embedding cost ~0; cache survives process restarts; eviction policy documented.
notes: Implemented persistent content-addressed embed_cache in crates/ast-sgrep-core/src/store/sqlite.rs. Cache key is (blake3(render_chunk_text), versioned model_id); schema v2 includes deterministic bounded LRU eviction, malformed-row rejection, model/backend/dimension safety, cache persistence, mixed hit/miss reuse, default offline Auto semantic prelookup, and safe provider fallback behavior. Focused store suite: 12 passed, 0 failed, 32 filtered. Clippy ast-sgrep-core with neural-embed,rerank and -D warnings passes. Release-perf CLI smoke across separate processes: cold two-symbol index 2 cache rows/0.02s; one-symbol doc edit yielded 3 rows and access generations [(1,1),(2,1),(3,1)], proving one miss plus one reused hit; unchanged warm reindex indexed 0/skipped 1 in 0.00s. Evidence committed to benchmarks/BASELINES.md.

title: Cold-start attack: mmap index, lazy segments, sub-100ms first query
description: Cold start is where streaming tools win by default. Make the index mmap-able with lazy segment loads so the first query pays only for the segments it touches, not full deserialization. Target: cold first-query overhead vs warm <100ms at 100k files. ACCEPTANCE: cold/warm split in the speed benchmark shrinks to the target; kill -9 safety preserved.
