title: Agent skill: use bd not br for this repo (Dolt backend)
description: AGENTS.md already says bd. Add one-line doctor check or README callout: br list shows 0 issues because backend is Dolt; run bd list. Prevents empty-backlog false alarm (2026-07-08 triage).
close_reason: Superseded by 2026-07-08 bd→br migration: AGENTS/CLAUDE/skill now use br; .beads gitignored. Inverse of original ask.

title: Learned fusion: per-language, per-query-class rank-fusion weights
description: Replace hand-set fusion constants with weights fit on the gold corpora (per-language x per-query-class), evaluated strictly held-out. Training is a committed, deterministic script; weights are versioned artifacts. ACCEPTANCE: held-out MRR >= fixed-fusion on every corpus; training reproducible from a clean checkout.
close_reason: done

title: NL-question retrieval eval + beat QMD as recall backend (capn-hook gap)
description: capn-hook's recall backend is QMD (github.com/tobi/qmd): semantic hybrid by default, ~300MB-2GB model download, cold ask takes seconds; BM25 fallback deterministic. Their measured retrieval: recall@k 60/60 but precision@1 only 56/60 on a 60-doc corpus — weak top-ranking at trivial scale. ast-sgrep opportunity: (1) build a natural-language-dev-question -> ground-truth-files eval corpus in their style (pinned OSS repos, hand-verified answers, precision@1 + recall@k + paraphrase robustness — the axis their eval explicitly skips); (2) benchmark ast-sgrep hybrid retrieval on it vs QMD BM25 and QMD hybrid as named competitors (bake-off culture: published losses included); (3) target: match or beat QMD hybrid precision@1 with zero model download and sub-second cold ask. Feeds wlp-embedder-quality-8370 (real local embeddings) — this corpus becomes its gate. Also positions ast-sgrep as the drop-in recall backend for any capn-style chart layer (see fszero-afo).
close_reason: done

title: graph retrieval: callers/imports normalization divergence — investigate
description:
Child of beads callers-imports-0-hits issue. Concrete debug steps:

1. Locate callers/imports query implementation in cli + core
2. Trace indexer write path for the same symbol form
3. Compare symbol canonicalization: lowercase, ASCII only, FQN, qualified
4. Confirm the divergence by inserting a fixture and printing actual stored keys vs query keys
5. Identify the fix surface (one side of normalization needs to align with the other) but do NOT apply yet
6. Document in PR-description draft

Should produce a small repro test or a concrete assertion failure that proves the issue.

Mode: read-only investigation. Mode stays read-only until parent issue is unblocked.
design: Investigation already largely captured in powt description. Close this by committing the fixture-backed evidence (stored vs queried key dump) so the divergence is reproducible for future refactors.
acceptance_criteria: Written comparison of stored key form (index write path) vs query key form (retrieval) for callers and imports, with an inserted fixture printing actual stored vs queried keys. The single normalization surface is named (symbol.rs prefixed_mode_query + rank.rs score_normalized_symbol). Finding linked to powt.

title: symbol canonicalization audit across codebase
description:
Broader audit: find every site in the Rust codebase that produces or consumes a symbol/key, document expected equivalence contracts, and surface divergences.

Sites to enumerate: rank.rs (score_symbol), query, semantic_ann.rs (fingerprint), semantic_ivf.rs (coherence hash), intent.rs (channel names), store/sql*.rs (TABLES / keys), LSP backend (symbol_resolution), plugins/capsule.rs (output formats).

Deliverable: a single doc (markdown) listing every site, the canonic form produced/consumed, and any cross-site divergence. No code changes. Produces a contract reference for future fixes.
design: Broad canonicalization contract audit. Output is a table site->contract->divergence. Feeds concrete fix beads (powt, z47q already found). Use this to define ONE canonical symbol-key normalization function reused everywhere.
acceptance_criteria: A written audit enumerates every site that produces or consumes a symbol/key (rank.rs, query.rs, semantic_ann.rs fingerprint, semantic_ivf.rs, intent.rs, store/sql*.rs, LSP backend, plugins/capsule) with each site documented equivalence contract (lowercase? ASCII-only? FQN? qualified?) and divergences flagged as follow-up beads. No code change in this task.

title: graph query oracle: parity test for defs/callers/imports/chain
description:
Add property-based test: index a small fixture, query every retrieval mode with a known symbol set, assert non-empty parity against indexed counts.

Catches the Issue #12 class of bug (data indexed but not retrievable) at test time. Without this oracle, the same class of bug can recur in any future refactor.

Acceptance: fixture indexed; defs/callers/imports/chain queries return matching hit counts above zero for at least N symbols; CI integration via cargo test --test graph_oracle.

Mode: read-only investigation first (write test plan + fixture spec), implementation later as separate beads.
design: This is the retrieval invertibility oracle (see also the frontier invertibility bead). Parametrize over lowercase, UPPER, camelCase, snake_case symbols and over languages. Depends on powt to pass.
acceptance_criteria: Property-based/parametrized test: index a small fixture, then for a known symbol set assert defs/callers/imports/chain each return counts consistent with the indexed counts (non-empty parity). Catches the data-indexed-but-not-retrievable class at test time. Runs in CI.

title: edge-case fall-through audit: query modes that short-circuit to empty
description:
Search the codebase for retrieval/query paths that may early-return empty due to edge cases (empty input, NaN/inf similarity, single-character symbols, ASCII-only assumption, Unicode normalization gap).

Pay attention to defs/callers/imports/chain/literal/word/pattern/semantic modes. Mode: read-only audit, output: list of candidate sites with reproduction rationale. No implementation until parent reviews.
design: Systematic short-circuit-to-empty audit. Cross-reference with found beads (powt, c2j5, hsv7). Produce candidate fix beads for any new sites.
acceptance_criteria: Read-only audit output: a list of retrieval/query paths that can early-return empty due to edge cases (empty input, NaN/inf similarity, single-char symbols, ASCII-only assumptions, Unicode normalization gaps) across defs/callers/imports/chain/literal/word/pattern/semantic, each with a reproduction rationale. No implementation.

title: F-01: prefixed_mode_query bypasses term lowercasing — callers:/defs:/imports: 0 hits on mixed-case (P0)
description: Root cause of Issue #12. ParsedQuery::parse lowercases via tokenize_for_scoring, but prefixed_mode_query (search/passes/symbol.rs:184-191) overwrites terms with vec![lookup_symbol()] which preserves target case. rank.rs:30-37 normalizes only the symbol side, not the term. score_normalized_symbol: "foo" == "Foo" -> false → score 0 → caller_rows_to_hits drops all rows. Affects LSP goto_definition and find_references too. chain works because calls_matching SQL uses lower() = lower(). Do NOT fix yet — read-only next step is to confirm with minimal repro.
design: Two candidate fix surfaces: (a) prefixed_mode_query builds terms via lookup_symbol().to_lowercase(); (b) score_normalized_symbol / normalized_symbol in rank.rs normalizes BOTH sides. Prefer (b): single normalization chokepoint so LSP (F-04/nuli) and chain inherit the fix automatically and no query path can regress. Keep tokenize_for_scoring semantics; do not change the lowercase-only fast path in normalized_symbol, extend it to the term argument.
acceptance_criteria: Minimal failing repro committed: on a mixed-case fixture, callers:RefreshToken and defs:RefreshToken return 0 while chain returns CalledBy edges (reproduces Issue #12). Root cause confirmed at search/passes/symbol.rs prefixed_mode_query. Fix makes symbol scoring case-insensitive on BOTH term and symbol sides at one chokepoint. After fix: callers:/defs:/imports: return non-zero on mixed-case AND lowercase symbols; rank ordering unit tests unchanged; chain parity preserved; new unit test asserts score_normalized_symbol(RefreshToken, refreshtoken) > 0; integration test green.
notes: Fixed on branch archive/wave-recovery (PR #13): score_normalized_symbol in rank.rs now normalizes BOTH the term and symbol sides (single chokepoint), so prefixed callers:/defs:/imports: and LSP inherit case-insensitive matching. Also merged a duplicate mod tests block in rank.rs that was breaking the test build. Regression tests: rank.rs symbol_scoring_is_case_insensitive_on_the_term_side (unit) + parity.rs prefixed_modes_are_case_insensitive_on_mixed_case_symbols (e2e). Full core+lsp suites green, clippy clean. Awaiting PR review/merge.
close_reason: F-01 root cause fixed + regression tests added on PR #13 (rank.rs term normalization). Core/LSP tests green, clippy clean.

title: F-02: SemanticCache + ANN fingerprint collide after delete+re-add with same max_id (P1)
description: cache hit uses max_id+lang_filter+embed_backend only. Fingerprint uses chunks.len()+max_id+dim+backend. File deleted then re-added can yield identical key with stale chunks/vectors. Fix: add monotonic data_version or content-hash. No code change.
design: search/mod.rs SemanticCache keys on max_id+lang+backend; semantic_ivf fingerprint keys on len+max_id+dim+backend. Add store data_version (already used by index_gen) into both so re-add with reused ids invalidates. Cheapest fix: thread index_gen into SemanticCache and compute_ann_fingerprint.
acceptance_criteria: A fixture that deletes then re-adds a file yielding identical (max_id, lang, backend) but different content no longer returns stale semantic hits. SemanticCache validity key includes a monotonic data_version (PRAGMA data_version) or content hash. ANN fingerprint likewise incorporates the same discriminator. Test: delete+re-add with changed body, assert fresh vectors served.

title: F-03: symbols_named case-sensitive vs calls_matching case-insensitive (P1)
description: symbols_named (store/sqlite.rs:762-769) WHERE s.name=?1 (case-sensitive). calls_matching uses lower()=lower(). In chain.rs:111-116, callee strings from outgoing_calls feed symbols_named — case mismatch silently drops chain nodes. Fix: case-insensitive in symbols_named. No code change.
design: store/sqlite.rs:762-769 uses WHERE s.name=?1. Change to lower(s.name)=lower(?1). Confirm an index on lower(name) or accept scan cost; measure on large fixture. Sibling of powt (same case-normalization class).
acceptance_criteria: symbols_named matches case-insensitively (lower()=lower()) consistent with calls_matching. chain.rs nodes whose callee case differs from the stored symbol name are no longer silently dropped. Test: fixture with camelCase caller feeding into symbols_named returns the node.

title: F-04: LSP find_references + goto_definition return empty on uppercase symbols (P1)
description: backend.rs:152-162 calls hit_locations(c, callers:{sym}) and defs:{sym}. Inherits F-01. Downstream fix or bypass to store APIs. No code change.
design: backend.rs:152-162 delegates to hit_locations with callers:{sym}/defs:{sym}, inheriting F-01. Preferred resolution: powt fix at rank.rs makes this pass with zero LSP changes. Add the LSP test regardless as a permanent guard.
acceptance_criteria: LSP find_references and goto_definition return non-empty results for uppercase/mixed-case symbols on a fixture. If powt fixes rank.rs chokepoint, this is validated by a new LSP integration test with no additional code change; otherwise backend.rs is bypassed to case-insensitive store APIs. Test asserts references(RefreshToken) non-empty.

title: F-05: literal_sql GLOB path escapes metachars (literal:arr[0] matches arr0) (P2)
description: passes/literal.rs:57-62 uses GLOB *{needle}* when small-index/short-needle. SQLite GLOB wildcards *, ?, [ ]. Treat needle pattern as wildcards. Fix: escape or use LIKE ESCAPE. No code change.
design: passes/literal.rs:57-62 builds GLOB *{needle}* for small-index/short-needle. Either escape [,],*,? per GLOB rules, or switch to LIKE with ESCAPE and %/_ escaping. Prefer LIKE ESCAPE for clarity.
acceptance_criteria: literal:arr[0] no longer matches arr0; SQLite GLOB metacharacters (*, ?, [, ]) in the needle are escaped or the query uses LIKE ... ESCAPE. Test: literal:a[b] matches a[b] literally and not axb. Existing plain-literal matches unchanged.

title: F-06: regex_pass loads all indexed lines into memory on regex without literal (P2)
description: passes/regex.rs:19-25 when required_literal returns None calls all_indexed_lines() loading whole corpus into Vec. OOM or seconds on large. Fix: streaming/FTS prefilter or line cap. No code change.
design: passes/regex.rs:19-25 calls all_indexed_lines() when required_literal is None. Options: (1) stream rows from SQLite in batches; (2) require a min-length literal or trigram prefilter; (3) hard cap lines scanned with a truncation warning. Prefer streaming batches to preserve correctness.
acceptance_criteria: regex_pass without an extractable required_literal no longer loads the entire corpus into memory. Either an FTS/line-cap prefilter bounds candidate lines, or scanning is streamed with a configurable cap. Benchmark on a large fixture shows bounded peak memory. Test asserts a literal-free regex still returns correct hits under the cap.

title: F-07: resolve_module_path is Rust-only; chain Imports unreliable for non-Rust (P2)
description: store/sqlite.rs:813-860 uses /src/ and :: for Rust. Python/JS/Go module paths unhandled. Fix: language-aware resolver (node_modules, __init__.py, Go mod paths). No code change.
design: store/sqlite.rs:813-860 hardcodes /src/ and :: (Rust). Add a language dispatch keyed on file language: JS/TS relative + node_modules resolution, Python package/module dotted path, Go import path vs module root. Keep Rust path as one branch.
acceptance_criteria: chain Imports edges resolve for Python, JS/TS, and Go fixtures, not only Rust. resolve_module_path is language-aware (node_modules / package.json, __init__.py packages, Go module paths). Test: per-language fixture asserts import edge target resolves to the correct file.

title: F-08: doc_comment_above treats # as comment for all languages (P2)
description: semantic_chunk.rs:72-87 strip_comment_marker includes # universally. #[derive(Debug)] in Rust/TS private fields #foo get injected into chunk text, polluting embeddings. Fix: language-aware comment marker set. No code change.
design: semantic_chunk.rs:72-87 strips # as a comment marker universally. Introduce a per-language comment-prefix table; only treat # as a line comment for languages where it is one. Guard against attribute/# sigil pollution.
acceptance_criteria: Rust attributes (#[derive(...)]) and TS/JS private fields (#foo) are no longer treated as doc comments and no longer injected into embedding chunk text. strip_comment_marker uses a language-aware marker set (# only for Python/Ruby/Shell/etc). Test: Rust chunk with #[derive] above a fn does not include the attribute as comment text.

title: F-09: OllamaEmbedder::dim() and CloudEmbedder::dim() return 0 (P2)
description: embedder.rs:172-175,196-199 return 0. allocators and pre-embed validators get 0 and may produce zero/empty vectors. Hidden by post-embed length detection. Fix: probe and cache, or assert at first embed. No code change.
design: embedder.rs:172-175,196-199 return 0. Probe dimension on first embed and cache in the struct (OnceCell), or assert non-zero at first embed. Currently masked by post-embed length detection; make it explicit to prevent zero/empty vector allocation.
acceptance_criteria: OllamaEmbedder::dim() and CloudEmbedder::dim() return the true embedding dimension (probed once and cached) rather than 0. Allocators and pre-embed validators receive correct dims. Test: dim() matches the length of an actual produced vector for each backend (mockable).

title: F-10: build_seeds correctness depends on score-sorted hits (P3)
description: chain.rs:71-84 early-exit on top_n assumes hits sorted by score desc from Searcher. No assertion. Future callers passing unsorted hits break seed selection. Fix: comment precondition or sort before iteration. No code change.
design: chain.rs:71-84 early-exits at top_n assuming score-desc order from Searcher. Cheapest robust fix: sort_by score desc at entry (hits are already small, top_n bounded). Alternatively document precondition + debug_assert.
acceptance_criteria: build_seeds no longer silently depends on caller-provided sort order: either it sorts hits by score desc before the top_n early-exit, or a debug_assert documents and checks the precondition. Test: passing unsorted hits yields the same seeds as sorted input.

title: F-11: exceeds_threshold ULP wrong for negative min (P3)
description: math.rs:8-19 f32::from_bits(min.to_bits()-1) is more negative for min<0, not less. Filter becomes too permissive. Latent (MIN_SIM=0.08 positive). Fix: clamp min>=0 or handle explicitly. No code change.
design: math.rs:8-19 uses f32::from_bits(min.to_bits()-1) which moves AWAY from zero for negative min (more negative = more permissive). Fix: for min<0 use from_bits(bits+1) direction, or clamp. Latent today because MIN_SIMILARITY=0.08>0; fix to prevent future negative thresholds.
acceptance_criteria: exceeds_threshold handles negative min correctly: the ULP nudge makes the bound strictly less permissive as intended for all sign values, or min is clamped >=0 with a documented rationale. Unit test covers min<0, min=0, min>0 boundaries.

title: F-12: body_structure_hash treats * as trivia universally (P3)
description: index.rs:395-410 trivia check includes line.starts_with(*). Breaks Go *Type pointers and *Rust raw-deref lines. Causes refresh_lines_only false-positive → stale symbols/callers/imports. Fix: only inside open block comment, or language-aware. No code change.
design: index.rs:395-410 trivia check includes line.starts_with('*') to skip block-comment continuation. Restrict to lines inside an open block comment, or make language-aware. False positives cause stale graph rows after edits.
acceptance_criteria: body_structure_hash no longer treats a leading * as trivia universally: Go *Type and Rust raw-deref/pointer lines are not misclassified, preventing false-positive refresh_lines_only that leaves stale symbols/callers/imports. Test: a Go file with *Type line changing body triggers full re-extraction.

title: F-13: ResponseCache no eviction at 128 entries (P3)
description: search/mod.rs:69-87 cap=128 without eviction. Long-lived Searcher (MCP) loses cache benefit after 128 distinct. Fix: LRU or oldest-evict. No code change.
design: search/mod.rs:69-87 caps map at 128 with no eviction (new inserts are simply dropped once full). Replace with a small LRU (indexmap or manual ring) keyed by (kind,query); keep gen-based full invalidation.
acceptance_criteria: ResponseCache evicts under a bounded policy (LRU or oldest-first) so a long-lived Searcher (MCP server) retains cache benefit beyond 128 distinct queries. Benchmark shows steady-state hit rate does not collapse to zero after 128 unique queries. Test: 200 distinct queries then re-query the most recent, assert cache hit.

title: F-14: Neural embedder silent fallback to Semantic with only eprintln (P4)
description: embedder.rs:298-302 --neural-embed falls through to Semantic on unavailability. User gets hashed embeddings silently. Fix: log WARN. No code change.
design: embedder.rs:298-302 falls through to Semantic with only eprintln. Promote to a structured WARN and record the effective backend in meta so status reflects the real backend.
acceptance_criteria: When --neural-embed is requested but unavailable, the fallback to Semantic emits a clear WARN (not a silent eprintln) and is surfaced in status/doctor so users know hashed embeddings are in use. Test: force neural-unavailable, assert warning emitted and backend recorded as fallback.

title: F-15: no integration test for callers:/defs:/imports: with mixed-case symbols (P4)
description: The exact gap that allowed Issue #12 to persist. Add fixture + assertions. No code change yet.
design: The exact missing test that let Issue #12 persist. Add under the core/LSP integration suite. Assert parity between status counts and query hits for at least one uppercase and one camelCase symbol.
acceptance_criteria: Integration test indexes a fixture containing mixed-case symbols and asserts callers:/defs:/imports: each return non-zero hits matching the indexed counts. This is the permanent guard for the Issue #12 class. Test fails on current main (pre-powt) and passes after the fix.

title: AST pipeline performance: reduce serial fractions (Amdahl) and remove threshold discontinuities
description: Umbrella for AST/search pipeline performance. Framing: (1) Amdahl's Law — identify and shrink the serial fraction of each hot path so added cores actually help; (2) remove hard threshold cliffs (the file_count>=128 bifurcation, the ANN=2000 cliff) in favor of continuous cost-model scaling; (3) an invertibility invariant that guarantees everything indexed stays retrievable (generalization of Issue #12). Children hold the concrete measured work. All require a reproducible benchmark (crates/ast-sgrep-core/src/bench_suite.rs) before/after.
design: Establish a stable benchmark harness first; attribute measured share of total time per stage so Amdahl bounds are real, not assumed.
acceptance_criteria: Each child is closed only with a before/after benchmark on a fixed corpus (wall time, peak RSS, and for search p50/p95 latency). No speculative optimization without a measured hot path.

title: PERF: pipeline index_all DB writes concurrently with parse/prep (Amdahl serial-fraction cut)
description: index.rs index_all parallelizes file prep via rayon par_iter (prepare_file), then writes the SQLite store SEQUENTIALLY inside one begin_bulk_tx/commit_bulk_tx loop. The write loop is the serial fraction: by Amdahl, if writes are a share S of total time, max speedup is bounded by 1/S regardless of cores. Overlap prep and write so the CPU parses the next batch while a writer thread drains committed rows.
design: Producer/consumer: rayon prep stage feeds a bounded channel; a single writer thread owns the SQLite connection and the bulk transaction (SQLite writers are single-threaded). Batch upserts (e.g. 256 files) to amortize tx overhead; consider WAL + synchronous=NORMAL. Keep prune_missing_files after the drain.
acceptance_criteria: Benchmark index_all on a fixed multi-thousand-file corpus before/after. After: wall-clock improves and the sequential write phase no longer dominates; row counts and content hashes are byte-identical to the current serial path; crash/rollback semantics preserved (partial batch still rolls back).

title: PERF: parallelize IVF kmeans assignment step (semantic_ann.rs)
description: semantic_ann.rs kmeans runs 12 iterations; each iteration reassigns every vector to its nearest centroid in a sequential loop (O(n*k*dim)) then recomputes centroids sequentially. For large corpora the assignment loop is the serial hot spot of IVF build. Parallelize assignment with rayon (par_iter over vectors) and reduce centroid sums in parallel.
design: Assignment is embarrassingly parallel: par_iter vectors -> nearest_centroid. Centroid recompute: parallel fold into per-thread sum/count then merge. Keep farthest-point seeding sequential (cheap). Guard determinism by stable reduction order or accept tolerance validated by recall.
acceptance_criteria: Benchmark IVF build on a large embedded corpus before/after. After: build wall-time drops with core count; final assignment is deterministic-equivalent given the same seed (or within tolerance); ANN recall unchanged on a fixed query set.

title: PERF: replace hard file_count>=128 search bifurcation with continuous cost-model (Mobius continuum)
description: search/mod.rs bifurcates at PARALLEL_PASS_FILE_THRESHOLD=128: below it passes run serially; at/above it search_hybrid_large spawns 4 thread::scope workers each opening its own IndexStore. This is a discontinuity: 127 vs 129 files take very different paths and the 4-worker fan-out has fixed cost that can lose on medium inputs. Replace the boolean cliff with one continuous decision (Mobius framing: a single continuous surface) that scales worker count by estimated cost (corpus size, enabled passes, embed on/off).
design: Model per-pass cost from store.status (file_count, symbol/caller counts) and options (use_embed, use_rerank). worker_count = clamp(estimated_parallel_work / per_thread_overhead). Reuse a connection pool instead of opening a fresh IndexStore per worker to cut fan-out fixed cost. Fall back to serial when estimated work < overhead.
acceptance_criteria: Microbenchmark across corpus sizes spanning the old threshold (32,64,128,256,1024 files) before/after. After: no latency regression on either side of the old 128 boundary; medium corpora do not pay unnecessary thread/store-open overhead; worst case never slower than today; identical hits to current code.

title: PERF: IVF candidate_indices selects top probes via select_nth instead of full centroid sort
description: semantic_ann.rs candidate_indices sorts ALL centroids by similarity (O(k log k)) then takes the top take (adaptive sqrt(k)). Only the top take are needed so partial selection (select_nth_unstable) is O(k). Small but on the hot query path for every ANN search.
design: Replace full sort with select_nth_unstable_by at index take-1 then iterate the top slice. Members are subsequently sorted/unique anyway. Low-risk; included because it is on every semantic query.
acceptance_criteria: Microbenchmark candidate selection for k in 16..256 before/after. After: fewer comparisons, identical candidate set membership (order within probes irrelevant since members are re-scored and sorted downstream); ANN recall unchanged.

title: PERF: batch chain BFS store queries per depth level (chain.rs)
description: chain.rs graph traversal expands nodes with per-node store queries as it walks depth levels, issuing many small SQL round-trips. Batch all expansions at a given depth into a single IN-list query to cut round-trips from O(nodes) to O(depth).
design: Collect the frontier set at each BFS depth, issue one IN-list query (chunked to SQLite variable limits), map results back. Preserve decay_factor scoring and dedup. Coordinate with z47q (case-insensitive symbols_named) so batched lookups do not drop mixed-case callees.
acceptance_criteria: Benchmark chain over the large indexed graph (144959-caller corpus) before/after. After: fewer SQL statements executed (assert via counter), lower wall time at depth>=2, identical node/edge sets to the per-node path.

title: FRONTIER: index<->query invertibility invariant (inverse-Jacobian round-trip guarantee)
description: Generalization of Issue #12 (data indexed at count 144959 but 0 retrievable). Framing via the inverse-Jacobian idea: the index transform T maps source symbols/callers/imports into stored keys; retrieval is T^-1. Issue #12 is exactly a point where T^-1 fails to recover what T stored (non-invertible on mixed-case input). Enforce an invariant: for every entity written by the indexer there exists a query in its mode (defs/callers/imports) that returns it. Encode as a property test that fuzzes symbol casing/unicode/language and asserts write-set == retrievable-set.
design: Theoretical closure of Issue #12: assert the round-trip law (index then query = identity on the key set) instead of patching one casing bug. Share the normalization chokepoint fixed in powt. Generalizes the parity oracle (55hl) from fixed fixtures to generated inputs. Any future normalization divergence breaks this test.
acceptance_criteria: A property-based test generates symbols across casings (lower/UPPER/camel/snake), scripts (ASCII + non-ASCII), and languages; indexes them; asserts every written symbol/caller/import is retrievable via its query mode with matching counts. Fails on pre-powt main, passes after normalization is fixed. Runs in CI as the permanent anti-regression gate for the whole class.

title: perf(rank): hoist query-term normalization out of per-row scoring loop
design: score_normalized_symbol normalizes the term on every call. In caller_rows_to_hits the same parsed.terms are scored against ~144k caller rows, so identical terms are re-normalized once per row. Normalize parsed.terms once per query (before the row loop) and pass pre-normalized terms into best_symbol_score/score_caller. Keep score_symbol's single-term public path normalizing internally. Validate with benches/search.rs (coverage_symbol_score) to confirm no regression and measure the win.
acceptance_criteria: Query-term normalization happens once per query, not once per scored row; benches/search.rs shows no regression (ideally improvement) on the caller-heavy path; all rank unit tests + parity e2e still pass; clippy + fmt clean.
